title: EVICTION PREVENTION - 211info
text: EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO EVICTION PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with past due rent payments. Assistance is limited to available funding and is not guaranteed. Cannot assist individuals who have Section 8. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Individuals and families who meet all of the following requirements: -Are a resident of Multnomah County -Are at substantial risk of experiencing homelessness -Are at or below 80% of area median income (AMI) Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm by appointment Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 669-8350 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information and to check if the waitlist is open FEES: None DOCUMENTS: -Identification for all adults over the age of 18 such as a driver's license, state ID card, Social Security card, passport, or birth certificate -Proof of income such as a pay stub or award letter for Supplemental Security Income (SSI), Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI), child support, or TANF. A personal statement may also be accepted on a case by case basis. -Copy of eviction notice or rental agreement LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Rent Payment Assistance Rent Payment Assistance * Hispanic/Latino Community Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - FOOD JUSTICE HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY RENT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - HOME FINDER SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH AND FAMILY EDUCATION ENGAGEMENT PREK-12 INFORMATION SERVICES - INFORMATION AND REFERRAL LEGAL SERVICES - FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - UNICA UTILITIES - ENERGY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO
